entries.presented: Integration scan + Q1 (existing reference codebase to reverse-engineer?) and Q2 (how Kiro subscription is accessed programmatically)
entries.decision: Q1: No — fully greenfield. Q2: c — treat Kiro inference access as a pluggable abstraction, resolve during design.

entries.presented: Final 8-stage workflow (requirements-analysis, story-generation, domain-design, units-generation, functional-design, nfr-design, infrastructure-design, code-generation); reviews on; supervised autonomy; skipped reverse-engineering, wireframe-design, contract-design
entries.decision: approved

entries.presented: Plan summary + 8 clarification questions (Q1-Q8) with options, trade-offs, and recommendations
entries.decision: Q1:a (dedicated channels), Q2:a (@mention), Q3:b (thread-scoped memory), Q4:a+b+c+d (ALL question types in scope — scope expansion), Q5:c (detailed + citations), Q6:b (ack then answer, p95<=30s default), Q7:b+c (internal-only policy + secret detection/refusal), Q8:a+b (adoption + thumbs feedback)

entries.presented: Final requirements.md (21 FRs, 10 NFRs, 9 assumptions incl. A-1 Kiro inference risk, 6 out-of-scope); Product Lead verdict READY with 3 non-blocking findings; noted all 21 FRs are v1 due to Q4 scope expansion
entries.decision: approved

entries.presented: 2 shaping questions (Q1 operator persona, Q2 metrics consumption scope)
entries.decision: Q1:a (lightweight Platform Operator persona for config/governance stories), Q2:a (capture-only this release, reporting deferred)

entries.presented: Final stories.md (27 stories, core-first ordering, full FR/NFR coverage matrix) + personas.md (Developer + Platform Operator); S-19 widened for worker-crash recovery; Product Lead verdict READY
entries.decision: approved

entries.presented: 6 component-decomposition questions (granularity, secret-scanner placement, async job lifecycle, shared-state ownership, cost-guardrail enforcement, config ownership)
entries.decision: agree to all — balanced 8-component catalogue CMP-001..008 (Slack Adapter, Agent Orchestrator, Inference Provider, MCP Client, Input Safety Scanner, Job Coordinator, Operational Data Service, Configuration & Policy)

entries.presented: Final components.yaml/components.md (8 components CMP-001..008, 14 single-owned entities, acyclic graph, correlation-id across async boundary); Architecture Reviewer verdict READY with minor F-1 doc fix
entries.decision: approved (F-1 coverage-matrix correction for S-23/NFR-7 applied on approval)

entries.presented: 4 packaging questions (unit grouping, team structure, independent scaling, inference-provider module boundary)
entries.decision: Q1:a (single deployable unit), Q2:a (one team), Q3:b (want independent worker scaling — tension with Q1a, owner to reconcile via in-unit horizontal worker scaling), Q4:a (Inference Provider as isolated buildable module, not separate unit). contract-design remains skipped.

entries.presented: Final units artifacts: single UNIT-001 (single deployable artifact, multi-role runtime), durable internal queue, isolated inference module, all 27 stories mapped, contract-design skipped; Architecture Reviewer verdict READY
entries.decision: approved — inception phase complete, proceed to construction for UNIT-001

entries.presented: 4 functional-shape questions for UNIT-001 (api-spec scope, ProcessingJob terminals, de-dup identity, feedback recording)
entries.decision: go with recommendations — Q1:c (both external + internal interfaces), Q2:b (distinct resolved|failed terminals), Q3:a (channel-id+message-ts de-dup key), Q4:b (append-only feedback log)

entries.presented: Final UNIT-001 functional artifacts (14 entities, 27 rules incl. BR-027 single-writer leasing, api-spec, 5 workflows, state machine); reviewer NOT-READY then READY after fixing a corrupted entity edit (1 review iteration); C-1 traceability fix applied on approval
entries.decision: approved

entries.presented: 5 NFR threshold/posture questions (latency, cost guardrail, availability, secret-detection posture, oversize handling) + noted tech stack Python 3.12 + Slack Bolt as architecture authority
entries.decision: go with recommendations — Q1:a (p95<=30s + 15s heartbeat), Q2:b (per-request cap + per-period soft budget), Q3:a (business-hours ~99% dependency-bounded), Q4:b (hard-refuse+re-ask naming pattern class, no override, NFR-4 preserved), Q5:c (hybrid trim/reject oversize). Tech stack Python 3.12 + Slack Bolt accepted.

entries.presented: Final nfr.yaml/nfr-spec.md (NFR-1..21 with concrete targets, verification, resilience patterns, tech stack); reviewer NOT-READY then READY after fixing 2 traceability defects (1 review iteration)
entries.decision: approved

entries.presented: 6 infrastructure questions (cloud/region/account, A-1 inference backend, compute runtime, queue+datastore, MCP connectivity, IaC+observability) + kiro-gateway API/hosting findings + AGPL-3.0 licensing flag
entries.decision: Q1:a (AWS single region/account); Q2: BOTH Kiro (via self-hosted kiro-gateway OpenAI-compatible proxy, primary) + Bedrock (alternate) behind CMP-003; Q3:a (serverless bot + container-hosted gateway); Q4:a (SQS+DynamoDB); Q5:a (hosted remote MCP over HTTPS); Q6:a (Terraform+CloudWatch+Secrets Manager). AGPL-3.0 of kiro-gateway recorded as OPEN licensing risk to confirm before code-generation (not silently accepted).

entries.presented: Final infrastructure-specification.md + infra-mapped components.yaml/unit.md (AWS serverless bot + Fargate kiro-gateway, dual-backend CMP-003, SQS+DynamoDB, EventBridge reaper, Terraform/CloudWatch/Secrets Manager); reviewer NOT-READY then READY after fixing 4 substantive findings (1 review iteration); F8 + AGPL carried to code-gen
entries.decision: approved — all design stages complete

entries.presented: Pre-code-gen AGPL-3.0 gate: how to treat the inference backend (a=Kiro-gateway primary+Bedrock alt, deploy gateway unmodified; b=Bedrock primary, Kiro adapter unshipped; c=accept AGPL fully; d=pause for legal review)
entries.decision: a — Kiro-gateway primary + Bedrock alternate; deploy gateway UNMODIFIED to bound AGPL copyleft exposure; AGPL accepted as internal tool. Code-gen builds the dual-backend CMP-003 with Kiro-gateway primary.

entries.presented: 4 code-gen scope/strategy questions (pass scope, F8 mapping persistence, AGPL boundary, test strategy)
entries.decision: go with recommendations — Q1:a (app+tests first, Terraform deferred), Q2:a (GSI on ProcessingJob by answer-message-ts), Q3:a (Kiro-gateway HTTP-client-only, gateway unmodified/external), Q4:a (moto + port fakes + injected clocks)

entries.presented: Final UNIT-001 application code + tests at workspace root (8 components, 3 Lambda entrypoints, dual-backend CMP-003); quality gate green (ruff/mypy/bandit/96 tests/88% cov); contributor caught + fixed HIGH adoption-metric defect; Code Reviewer verdict READY; Terraform IaC + NFR-11 heartbeat wiring tracked as follow-ons
entries.decision: approved — UNIT-001 construction complete, intent-001-slack-devops-agent complete

entries.presented: Offer to run tracked follow-ons (Terraform IaC slice, NFR-11 heartbeat wiring, BR-027 repost-window hardening)
entries.decision: yes, run all — proceed with all follow-on increments (Terraform authored + validated only, never applied)

entries.presented: Follow-on increment: Terraform IaC (9 modules + gateway autoscale 2->4, F8 GSI, least-privilege IAM, fmt/validate only), NFR-11 heartbeat wiring, BR-027 pre-post intent hardening; architect found+fixed gateway autoscaling defect; Code Reviewer READY; gate green (105 tests, 88% cov, terraform validate)
entries.decision: approved — all follow-ons closed; intent-001-slack-devops-agent fully delivered with no open follow-ons
